mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS fail to coincide with positive average 20-day SPX forward returns over any 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, restrictive monetary policy materially raises the equity discount rate and dampens credit-sensitive spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS > 4%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative on average (mean return >= 0) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and a high federal funds rate jointly compresses real corporate margins and household purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that exceed either single-factor effect.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS exhibits a Rank IC greater than -0.03 or is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during late-cycle expansions signals impending demand contraction and earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; outside late-cycle phases this relationship weakens materially.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, increases in UNRATE fail to coincide with negative average 60-day SPX forward returns (mean return >= 0 over triggered instances) or the signal hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts below 1,350 thousand units foreshadows weakness in construction, durable goods, and household wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1350, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals improving manufacturing activity, inventory replenishment, and rising capacity utilization, which drives positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in INDPRO show non-positive average 5-day SPX forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in consumer spending and labor income reinforces aggregate demand momentum, producing amplified positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed either single-series effect in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to produce positive average 20-day SPX forward returns, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth in an environment of elevated policy rates signals a policy-induced demand recession, leading to sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations rapidly reset lower.
failure_condition: When triggered, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative on average over a 2-year rolling window, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not below zero.
